hero_subtitle: SFTY connects sanctions screening outputs into a continuous onboarding and monitoring workflow that preserves audit trails and supports risk-based decision-making for digital asset businesses.

problem_description: Compliance teams face delays and manual effort when sanctions rescreening is scattered across multiple providers and systems. This fragmentation reduces the clarity and traceability of risk signals during onboarding and ongoing due diligence for VASPs.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY integrates multiple sanctions providers’ outputs into a unified compliance workflow that supports ongoing rescreening, risk assessment, case creation and audit-ready evidence for VASP compliance teams.
workflow_title: From sanctions alerts to case evidence
workflow_description: SFTY routes sanctions rescreening alerts through risk review, case creation, decision logging and evidence preservation for controlled compliance workflows.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond fragmented checks. One audit-ready layer

why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view

authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow

ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY support ongoing sanctions rescreening workflows
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how sanctions screening outputs connect to case reviews

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: How does SFTY support sanctions rescreening?
faq_1_answer: SFTY connects sanctions screening outputs into ongoing monitoring workflows, enabling structured rescreening, risk-based review and audit-ready evidence across customer and corporate onboarding processes.
faq_2_question: How are sanctions checks integrated in workflows?
faq_2_answer: Sanctions checks feed into risk-based workflows that trigger case creation, review routing, alert escalation and decision logging to support consistent sanctions rescreening throughout customer lifecycle stages.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve sanctions rescreening evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves decision logs, review history and case evidence related to sanctions rescreening to provide an auditable trail for compliance and regulatory inspection readiness.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY connect existing sanctions providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY integrates outputs from multiple sanctions providers, orchestrating their results within one connected compliance workflow without replacing any underlying screening service.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace current sanctions screening tools?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY acts as a compliance control layer above screening tools and connects their outputs without replacing the existing sanctions screening providers.
final_title: Structure sanctions rescreening into controlled workflows
final_description: Review how SFTY connects sanctions outputs into ongoing monitoring, risk-based review and audit-ready evidence for VASP compliance teams.
